message:
feat(mvcc): add merge-proof resolution to allow non-conflicting concurrent writes to same block

Introduce MergeProof, a caller-supplied proof that concurrent writes to
the same block are semantically compatible and can be 3-way merged
rather than rejected by First-Committer-Wins (FCW) validation.

Supported merge strategies:
- AppendOnly: both writers extend a shared prefix
- IndependentKeys / NonOverlappingExtents / TimestampOnlyInode:
  writers touch disjoint byte ranges within the block
- DisjointBlocks / Unsafe: no merge attempted (existing behavior)

When FCW detects a conflict, the system attempts merge resolution via
MergeProof::merge_bytes(base, latest, staged) before aborting. Applied
to both MvccStore and ShardedMvccStore (FCW and SSI paths), journal
commit in ffs-core, and WAL commit in persist.rs.

Transaction gains merge_proofs map via stage_write_with_proof(). Sharded
store refactored with extracted helpers (preflight_fcw_locked,
install_committed_version_locked, etc.) to support merge in both paths.

Includes tests for append-only merging, disjoint-range merging,
100-concurrent-writer stress, and unsafe-writer-conflict validation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
